issues.detail: Example has no annotations array — annotations missing entirely
Example has no annotations array — annotations missing entirely
gloss_hint 'pilot' on 'Piloten' should be 'pilotfish' to match sense 4 (pilotfish/marine). This example is about catching a fish, not an aircraft pilot
gloss_hint 'pilot' on 'Piloten' should be 'apple (pilot)' to match sense 8 (apple variety). The sentence is about loading hundreds of Piloten onto a truck — refers to apples, not people
gloss_hint on 'Piloten' is null but this example is for sense 10 (piling/construction). Should have gloss_hint 'piling' to disambiguate from the 12 other senses
Translation says 'He threw the pilot to his colleague' but this is sense 11 (remote control/electrical). Should be 'He threw the remote control to his colleague.'
Example has no annotations array — annotations missing entirely
gloss_hint 'emotional attachment' on 'Bindung' matches sense 2 but this example is under sense 1 (bond). gloss_hint should be 'bond' to match sense 1

All annotations are English-text annotations ('He', 'is', 'a', 'good', 'runner') — LLM annotated the translation instead of the German sentence. Should annotate German forms: Er, ist, ein, guter, Läufer
All annotations are English-text annotations ('Send', 'me', 'the', 'documents', etc.) — LLM annotated the translation instead of the German sentence. Should annotate: Schickt, Unterlagen, Läufer
Multiple English-text annotations: 'Runner' with English lemma 'runner', 'Schlafzimmer' with English lemma 'bedroom'. German lemmas should be used: Läufer (with gloss_hint 'runner rug'), Schlafzimmer
gloss_hint 'work as linotype' on 'durchschlug' is wrong. 'sich durchschlagen' here means 'to scrape by/manage' (sense 11). gloss_hint should match that sense or be null
All annotations use English lemmas ('visit', 'underground', 'be', 'popular', 'drive', 'two', etc.). LLM annotated the translation text. All should use German forms and lemmas
Lemma for 'zieht' is 'ziehen' but should be 'aufziehen' (separable verb). Also gloss_hint 'draw' is wrong — should relate to 'line up' (sense 6)
Translation 'One can draw maps on corkboards' is incorrect. 'aufziehen' in sense 9 (stretch over) means to mount/stretch, not draw. Should be: 'One can mount maps on cork boards.'
Translation 'I had to put the sweater back on' is wrong. This is sense 10 (unpick/unravel). Should be: 'I had to unravel the sweater again.'
First annotation 'Sister' with lemma 'sister'/noun is English-text annotation. Should be 'Schwester' with lemma Schwester/noun. Also 'Sie' has gloss_hint 'she' but here it is the formal 'you' (Sie)
Annotation uses English lemma: 'Kanton' has lemma 'canton' instead of German 'Kanton'
All annotations are English-text ('court', 'Netherlands', 'consists', 'four', 'sectors', etc.). LLM annotated the translation instead of the German sentence
Only annotation is English-text: 'away' with lemma 'away'/adverb. LLM annotated the translation. Should annotate German forms: ist, nächsten, Montag, weg
gloss_hint 'draw' on 'zeichnet' is wrong for this sense. This is sense 11 'show (condition)' — hunting term where game shows signs of being hit. gloss_hint should be 'show (condition)'
Translation 'I have worked through it on the wing' is wrong. This is sense 3 (grand piano). Should be: 'I have worked through it on the grand piano.'
gloss_hint 'wing (anatomy)' on 'Flügel' is wrong — this example is under sense 3 (grand piano). Should be 'grand piano'
Example has no annotations array — annotations missing entirely
Example has no annotations array — annotations missing entirely
gloss_hint on 'Flügel' is a pipe-delimited list of all 10 senses instead of a single disambiguating hint. Should be 'wing (formation)' to match sense 7

gloss_hint 'wing (anatomy)' on 'Flügel' is wrong — this is sense 10 (wing/symmetry, altar wings). Should be 'wing (symmetry)'. Also 'ist' has gloss_hint 'we lost' and 'gegangen' has gloss_hint 'they lost' — both wrong, should be null
gloss_hint 'wing (building)' on 'Flügel' is wrong — this is sense 10 (wing/symmetry, window shutters). Should be 'wing (symmetry)'
All annotations are English-text ('The', 'sky', 'was', 'orange', 'to', 'violet'). LLM annotated the translation instead of the German sentence. Should annotate: Himmel, orange, violett
All annotations are English-text ('I', 'often', 'work', '50', 'to', '60', 'hours', 'a', 'week'). LLM annotated the translation instead of the German sentence. Should annotate: arbeite, Stunden, Woche

All annotations are English-text ('The', 'parliament', 'consists', 'of', 'up', 'to', '320', 'members'). LLM annotated the translation instead of the German sentence. Should annotate: Parlament, besteht, Abgeordneten
'Mutter' has English lemma 'mother' instead of German lemma 'Mutter'
gloss_hint 'leftover' on 'Ohren' is wrong — 'Ohr' means 'ear'. gloss_hint should be null or 'ear'
'weiß' has lemma 'weiß'/adjective ('white') but in 'wer weiß welche' it is the verb 'wissen' (to know). Should be lemma 'wissen'/verb

Translation 'He has snapped the film except for the last picture' uses 'except for' but 'bis auf' here means 'down to' (inclusive, sense 9). He used up the film down to the last picture. Should be: 'He has snapped the film down to the last picture.'
'ich' has pos 'noun' with gloss_hint 'we | I (pronoun) | ego' — should not be annotated (pronouns are not content words)
